frameworks.name: uncertainty-guided exploration
frameworks.one_line: explore-exploit decisions driven by distinct uncertainty signals

frameworks.name: directed exploration (uncertainty bonus)
frameworks.one_line: relative uncertainty adds a bonus that biases choice toward less-known options

frameworks.name: random exploration (stochastic choice)
frameworks.one_line: total uncertainty scales decision noise to encourage exploration

frameworks.name: model-based fMRI of two-armed bandit
frameworks.one_line: computational model fits to bandit choices regressed against BOLD

core_question: Are directed and random exploration supported by dissociable neural representations of different uncertainty signals?
key_assumptions: two uncertainty quantities (relative and total) can be independently manipulated
exploration strategies combine additively in a hybrid decision policy
model-derived uncertainty estimates index neural computation
manipulations: relative uncertainty between two bandit options
total uncertainty across the two options
key_findings: relative uncertainty correlates with BOLD in right rostrolateral prefrontal cortex
total uncertainty correlates with BOLD in left dorsolateral prefrontal cortex
right RLPFC signal drives directed exploration via uncertainty bonus
subjective uncertainty estimates from both regions predict subsequent choices
interpretations: different uncertainty computations are performed in dissociable prefrontal regions
a hybrid architecture combines these signals downstream to guide exploration
